pattern: Flatten the grid into a row-major and a column-major string, find every pattern occurrence with KMP, then mark the covered cells in each orientation and count their intersection.
hints: Reading the grid left-to-right with wrap is just the cells in row-major order; reading top-to-bottom with wrap is the cells in column-major order. Each is one long string.
Find all start positions of the pattern in each long string. A match starting at flat index s covers the cells at flat indices s, s+1, ..., s+len(pattern)-1.
Map each flat index back to an (r, c) cell. Keep a set of cells covered horizontally and a set covered vertically, then count cells present in both.

edge_inputs.description: single cell matching a length-1 pattern is covered both ways
single cell that does not match the pattern
horizontal match wraps across a row boundary but vertical does not align there
pattern present horizontally but never vertically yields zero overlap
uniform grid so every cell is covered both ways for a short pattern
pattern equals the whole row-major / column-major string only one way
overlapping occurrences of the pattern in one orientation

stress.description: random small-alphabet grid with a short pattern so matches are dense in both orientations
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, size // 4))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size // 4))
    alpha = 'ab'
    grid = [[rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    plen = rng.randint(1, min(m * n, 5))
    pattern = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(plen))
    return [grid, pattern]
stress.note: small alphabet keeps occurrences frequent so the overlap logic is exercised
